Title: Collaborative Empiricism in Case Conceptualization
Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Know how to form and sustain a positive therapy alliance

Title: Collaborative Empiricism in Case Conceptualization
Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Communication skills: can communicate ideas clearly; can listen well and understand client communications; verbal and nonverbal expressions are congruent

Title: Collaborative Empiricism in Case Conceptualization
Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Understand the role of collaboration in CBT and how to collaborate to accomplish common tasks (e.g., agenda setting)

Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Recognize the importance of therapy structure, including knowledge of how to negotiate tasks within the therapy hour (e.g., time spent on various topics)

Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Understand how to use functional analysis and/or the five-part model

Title: Collaborative Empiricism in Case Conceptualization
Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Know methods to identify and rate emotions, thoughts, and physiological phenomena

Title: Collaborative Empiricism in Case Conceptualization
Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Understand how to engage clients in behavioral observation

Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Know the four stages of Socratic dialogue

Title: Collaborative Empiricism in Case Conceptualization
Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Have knowledge of cultural variations

Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Know basics of how to set up and debrief behavioral experiments

Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Know how to use Thought Records, prediction logs, and other written methods to observe and test beliefs

Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Understand how to use relevant questions to gather the information necessary to form a case conceptualization

Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Can present evidence-based models to client that fit with client-reported experiences

Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Can make predictions and employ behavioral experiments to evaluate case conceptualization

Title: Collaborative Empiricism in Case Conceptualization
Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Can navigate conflict and collaboratively work through therapy impasses

Title: Collaborative Empiricism in Case Conceptualization
Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Is able to attain and maintain collaboration with most clients, including those who have great difficulty collaborating in relationships

Title: Collaborative Empiricism in Case Conceptualization
Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Can create natural conversational bridges between client observations and empirically derived models

Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Is alert to subtleties in similarities and differences between client reports of experience and empirically supported models

Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Can use and/or seek data from client to reconcile differences between empirically supported models and client's personal experience

Title: Collaborative Empiricism in Case Conceptualization
Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Is able to weave empirical methods seamlessly into the fabric of therapy

Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Can readily use case conceptualization to navigate therapy impasses, evaluate treatment options, and plan for relapse management

Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Is able to reflect on and identify personal biases that interfere with empirical processes

Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Understands goal-setting processes

Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Can identify automatic thoughts, underlying assumptions, and core beliefs

Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Can identify triggers and maintenance factors

Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Recognizes safety behaviors

Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Familiar with expected treatment length and outcomes based on knowledge of relevant research with various clinical populations

Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Aware of personality types as well as common underlying assumptions and core beliefs associated with each

Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Knows how to integrate predisposing and protective factors within a longitudinal conceptualization

Recommended Knowledge Base and Competenc: Knows positive psychology literature and understands empirical bases of a strengths focus
